title: The Best Way to Get All 9 Dog Breeds in Minecraft

scenes.script_line: The Best Way to Get
scenes.clipper_notes: For this one and the next scene there will be 9 dogs (forming a 3by3 square of dogs), each of a different breed sitting in a group in a studio chess world. For this one have my player walking towards the camera infront of them.

scenes.script_line: All 9 Dog Breeds in Minecraft.
scenes.clipper_notes: Camera focused on just the dogs from #1

scenes.script_line: You're probably thinking
scenes.clipper_notes: Show Bart Simpsons character (no nametag just skin), standing still in the overworld (with some room above their head, see editor notes for context)

scenes.script_line: just tame one in each biome
scenes.clipper_notes: Will have 4a, 4b, 4c. Show my character taming a wolf in 3 different biomes with 3 different wolf types, try to have same relative angle so when it cycles through it seems clean

scenes.script_line: and bring them home.
scenes.clipper_notes: Show my player walking with a bunch of dogs on leashes, they dont need to be all 9 breeds but at least a couple different breeds

scenes.script_line: But wolves won't teleport to you
scenes.clipper_notes: 6 and 7 will have the same clip just two different angles. It will be my character next to a dog on the ground and my character ahs an elytra. Then, they fly away into the sky but the dog stays in the same spot. #6 is focus on the dog and my player but as my player flies away keep the camera on the dog

scenes.script_line: while you're flying with an elytra,
scenes.clipper_notes: Same clip from #6 but this time focused on my player using TrackEntity flying away with the elytra

scenes.script_line: and walking them across thousands of blocks is crazy
scenes.clipper_notes: This will have 8a and 8b. 8a will be my player walking in a chess studio world this time with dogs on leashes similar to scene #5, like focusing on my player walkning. Then 8b will be birds eye view from far above showing what my player is walking to like its far away, could have an X on the ground or something in the studio world showing the destination, when looking down, have my player be walking "up" / "north" so it fits well in a shorts screen.

scenes.script_line: there's no way they're all making it back safely...
scenes.clipper_notes: Show same area as #8 with walking the dogs in the studio world but now the dogs and my player are fighting some mobs.

scenes.script_line: But there's actually a way
scenes.clipper_notes: Show my player walking towards the camera infront of the scene from #9

scenes.script_line: to collect all 9 in a single trip
scenes.clipper_notes: Show the scene from #1 and #2 with all 9 dogs sitting and now have my player standing near the dogs shifting and unshifting

scenes.script_line: without losing half of them.
scenes.clipper_notes: Will have 12a and 12b. Show birds eye view of the dog grid. 12a is all the dogs are still there, then 12b is half of them are gone, its 9 so 4 or 5 can be removed here. In a way that the transition between the two is smooth, with camera in the same location between the two clips

scenes.script_line: And it involves building Noah's Ark.
scenes.clipper_notes: Show timelapse of my character building a simple boat, can be 5by5 blocks or so of just a hull of a boat, in studio world or overworld works here

scenes.script_line: Let me explain.
scenes.clipper_notes: My character looking into the camera infront of the scene from #13

scenes.script_line: In 1.21, Minecraft added the ability
scenes.clipper_notes: Show track entity clip of a 'Minecraft' named character, with a Mojang skin, just walking along.

scenes.script_line: to attach leads to boats.
scenes.clipper_notes: Show in the overworld next to an ocean but on land like on a beach or something. My player connecting a lead to a boat thats in the water.

scenes.script_line: But here's the important part
scenes.clipper_notes: Show my character talking to the camera infront of the boat from #17

scenes.script_line: you can also attach boats to other boats.
scenes.clipper_notes: Show my character attaching a boat to another boat with a lead.

scenes.script_line: So you create a fleet:
scenes.clipper_notes: I've attached a reference image for this. Basically the lead boat with boat and chest, then 3 lines of boats connecting to the first one in that specific formation. (Also this is all still in that one place near the water from the previous scenes)

scenes.script_line: a chest boat as your lead ship for supplies,
scenes.clipper_notes: Closer up on the lead ship, with my player sitting in it

scenes.script_line: then 9 boats in formation behind.
scenes.clipper_notes: Camera focused on the 9 behind

scenes.script_line: Then sail the coastline
scenes.clipper_notes: Show my player sailing the boat formation along a coastline

scenes.script_line: hitting every biome,
scenes.clipper_notes: 23a, 23b, 23c. Just show 3 different biomes (dont need to have my character or the boats in it), like one snow, desert, jungle etc. representing 3 of the biomes with dog breeds

scenes.script_line: pushing two dogs of each breed into one boat,
scenes.clipper_notes: Show timelapse of pushing two dogs of a breed into one of the boats.

scenes.script_line: they can't move or teleport from the boat
scenes.clipper_notes: Show the dogs pushed into the boat from #24 closer up, showing they're not moving

scenes.script_line: so they'll be completely safe.
scenes.clipper_notes: Show my player shifting and unshifting next to the dogs in the boat from #24 and #25 like they're happy the dogs are safe.

scenes.script_line: Once you have them all,
scenes.clipper_notes: Show each boat filled with 2 dogs of each breed, the formation just stationary for now.

scenes.script_line: you've officially created a carrier strike group of dogs in Minecraft.
scenes.clipper_notes: Show the dog formation moving across the open water.

scenes.script_line: But really,
scenes.clipper_notes: Show my character looking at the camera infront of the scene from #30

scenes.script_line: the best part isn't even just having the dogs.
scenes.clipper_notes: Show a different camera angle of all the dogs and their babies in the village, like a further away, somewhat-cinematic type shot if you can

scenes.script_line: It's that you can finally build 9 biome-themed doghouses
scenes.clipper_notes: You really just need to build 3 of these: 4by4 block 'doghouses' (including walls). See the later script: the kennel for sand dogs, (like made of sandstone), spruce cabin for taiga (just built from spruce wood), and an igloo for snowy wolves. Show all 9 in a row.

scenes.script_line: — a spruce cabin for the taiga dogs
scenes.clipper_notes: Just closer up on the taiga doghouse with 2 pale wolf variants

scenes.script_line: a sandstone kennel for the desert ones,
scenes.clipper_notes: Closer up on the sandstone dog house

scenes.script_line: and an igloo for the snowy wolves.
scenes.clipper_notes: Closer up on the igloo dog house

scenes.script_line: But now that I think about it,
scenes.clipper_notes: Show my character looking at the camera infront of the doghouses

scenes.script_line: getting every dog breed should totally be an achievement in the game,
scenes.clipper_notes: Show two 'cinematic' type shots of the dog houses and my player like walking around feeding them or something.

scenes.script_line: Mojang what are you doinngggggg?!?!?!?!
scenes.clipper_notes: Show my player jumping around in place and punching like they're frustrated / mad at mojang
